title: Arabis fendleri
intro: Arabis fendleri, also known as Fendler's rockcress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arabis fendleri include flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Fendler's rockcress is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil in herbal medicine, often used to treat coughs, bronchitis, and sore throats.

It is a member of the genus Arabis, which includes about 100 species of plants native to the Northern Hemisphere. Arabis fendleri is a perennial herb that is native to the western United States.
